page_content:
THET390 History of Theatre I (3 Credits) The history of Western theatre from its origins in classical antiquity through the mid-seventeenth century with emphasis on plays and playwrights, architecture and decor, acting and costuming, and signiﬁcant personalities. Includes explorations of interrelationships between Western theatre and the theatre of other cultures. Prerequisite: Permission of ARHU-School of Theatre, Dance & Performance Studies department.

THET391 Theatre History II (3 Credits) The history of Western theatre from the mid seventeenth century to the early twentieth century, with emphasis on plays and playwrights, architecture and decor, acting and costuming, and signiﬁcant personalities. Includes explorations of interrelationships between Western theatre and the theatre of other cultures. Prerequisite: Permission of ARHU-School of Theatre, Dance & Performance Studies department.

THET399 Independent Study (1-3 Credits) An independent study in which each student completes an assigned major theatre project under close faculty supervision. Projects may culminate in term papers, scenic; lighting, or costume designs; or a stage production. Restriction: Permission of instructor; and junior standing or higher. Repeatable to: 6 credits if content differs.
